testPurpose.objective: Verify four isolated users can create, join, retain fixed identities, complete one visible base-deck Criminal Dance match through legal UI actions, keep every exact hand and draw result isolated, and reach one consistent normalized terminal result.

successCriteria.description: All four tabs retain distinct fixed player identities for the entire match.
The host selects four players with Inspector and Juvenile disabled, and every tab visibly renders the same settings.
Exact hands, draws, received cards, forced discard choices, and actor-private card results appear only on the owning tab and in the owning agent Observation.
Every strategic card, target, cancel, confirm, and forced action is legal in the same player's visible UI and references that player's current Observation.
Seat order, current player, hand counts, played piles, public cards, scores, and public log remain consistent across all four tabs.
All four tabs reach the same score-to-10 normalized terminal result, regardless of winner, strategy quality, or match length.

testPurpose.recommendationRationale: Four players and the visible base deck are the requested lowest-risk first baseline. Disabling both expansions isolates the core loop. The fast profile removes only harness operation delay and resolves to production serverTimeScale 1.0; the game declares no scalable non-decision waits. No targeted scenario is forced, so all strategic cards and targets remain autonomous.
